rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Soco West, Inc.¿S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 13, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Soco West, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery on plaintiff seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiff¿s contentions against it, and that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden as to collateral estoppel. Since defendant did not attach responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant did not demonstrate what plaintiff¿s claims are as to it and thus did not demonstrate that any of the collateral estoppel requirements have been met. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GILBERT FORBES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 13, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant John K. Bice Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos supplied or distributed by defendant. Defendant¿s hearsay objections to the 3M documents are sustained. The 3M custodian was unable to provide any information about the person(s) who either wrote or provided the information about defendant¿s asserted supply or distribution of asbestos to 3M¿s facility, much less that such person(s) had knowledge of the relevant facts from personal observation. (Jazayeri v. Mao (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 301, 322 (¿The key to establishing the admissibility of a document made in the regular course of business is proof that the person who wrote the information or provided it had knowledge of the facts from personal observation.¿)). If this tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will take place before Judge Kahn at 1:30 pm in Department 302. Judge Kahn will hear this motion based on the parties¿ stipulation. Judge Kahn, who made the cross-outs on the stipulated order from September 22 without any request or prompting by anyone from counsel for the plaintiff, determined that the parties are entitled to have their stipulation regarding the judge who will hear the motion honored in this particular situation. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for the motion. = (503/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES HUSBAND VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 13, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue of material fact whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Although defendant did not file a separate evidentiary objection document, the court treated defendant¿s evidentiary arguments in its reply papers as objections. As such, defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled and its objections to Mr. Lamphiear¿s deposition testimony and Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WALTER MORRIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 13, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Nacco Materials Handling Group, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. As to the summary judgment motion, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and the deposition of Mr. Libby create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Hyster brakes) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff has dismissed his false representation cause of action and his claim for punitive damages, and those were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am in Dept. 503, not 9:30am in Dept. 220. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)
